name: lbd
title: lbd

commands.name: lbd

arguments.metavar: is equivalent to ANY
is like but omits RRSIG NSEC NSEC3
arguments.raw: -A is like -a but omits RRSIG, NSEC, NSEC3

arguments.metavar: specifies query class for non-IN data
arguments.raw: -c specifies query class for non-IN data

arguments.metavar: compares SOA records on authoritative nameservers
arguments.raw: -C compares SOA records on authoritative nameservers

arguments.metavar: is equivalent to
lists all hosts in a domain using AXFR
arguments.raw: -l lists all hosts in a domain, using AXFR

arguments.metavar: set memory debugging flag trace record usage
changes the number of dots allowed before root lookup is done
arguments.raw: -N changes the number of dots allowed before root lookup is done

arguments.metavar: specifies the port on the server to query
arguments.raw: -p specifies the port on the server to query

arguments.metavar: disables recursive processing
specifies number of retries for UDP packets
arguments.raw: -R specifies number of retries for UDP packets

arguments.metavar: a SERVFAIL response should stop query
arguments.raw: -s a SERVFAIL response should stop query

arguments.metavar: specifies the query type
enables TCP/IP mode
enables UDP mode
enables verbose output
print version number and exit
specifies to wait forever for a reply
arguments.raw: -w specifies to wait forever for a reply

arguments.metavar: specifies how long to wait for a reply
arguments.raw: -W specifies how long to wait for a reply

arguments.metavar: use IPv4 query transport only
use IPv6 query transport only

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# lbd -h
 host: illegal option -- h
 Usage: host [-aCdilrTvVw] [-c class] [-N ndots] [-t type] [-W time]
           [-R number] [-m flag] [-p port] hostname [server]
      -a is equivalent to -v -t ANY
      -A is like -a but omits RRSIG, NSEC, NSEC3
      -c specifies query class for non-IN data
      -C compares SOA records on authoritative nameservers
      -d is equivalent to -v
      -l lists all hosts in a domain, using AXFR
      -m set memory debugging flag (trace|record|usage)
      -N changes the number of dots allowed before root lookup is done
      -p specifies the port on the server to query
      -r disables recursive processing
      -R specifies number of retries for UDP packets
      -s a SERVFAIL response should stop query
      -t specifies the query type
      -T enables TCP/IP mode
      -U enables UDP mode
      -v enables verbose output
      -V print version number and exit
      -w specifies to wait forever for a reply
      -W specifies how long to wait for a reply
      -4 use IPv4 query transport only
      -6 use IPv6 query transport only

examples.command:
lbd example.com

lbd - load balancing detector 0.1 - Checks if a given domain uses load-balancing.
Written by Stefan Behte (http://ge.mine.nu)
Proof-of-concept! Might give false positives.

Checking for DNS-Loadbalancing: NOT FOUND
Checking for HTTP-Loadbalancing [Server]:
ECS (sea/55ED)
ECS (sea/1C15)
FOUND
lbd - load balancing detector 0.4 - Checks if a given domain uses load-balancing.

apt_metadata.description:
Load balancer detector
Checks if a given domain uses load-balancing.
